text: I feel a sense of duality in these spaces. The first image captures the calm anticipation of a liminal, almost sterile environment—a threshold between moments that invites quiet contemplation. The presence of another figure magnifies my own solitude as I observe from a distance, half-participant, half-witness. The second image is much livelier—immersed in the world of ideas and innovation, I am surrounded by symbols of curiosity and progress. Here, creativity floats in the air, making me feel both humbled and energized, as if the boundaries between human thought and technological possibility are blurred. In both places, I am an observer drawn to the subtle tensions between movement and stillness, past and future.
